items.headline: City to study eliminating chemical pesticides on public property
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will review a policy to stop using chemical pesticides on city-owned or leased land. This would affect parks, medians, and other public spaces Sunnyvale residents use regularly.

items.headline: Sunnyvale considers artificial turf policy for city property
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will study a policy on artificial turf use on city property. The outcome could shape what residents see in local parks and recreational areas.

items.headline: Echo Place development receives permits for 6 new homes and 2 duplexes
items.summary: Eight permits issued for the Miller development on Echo Place will add 10 residential units to the neighborhood. The project includes five single-family homes and two duplexes priced between $382,000 and $722,000.

items.headline: Lytton Avenue restaurant permitted to add outdoor dining area
items.summary: A restaurant at 407 Lytton Avenue received approval to add a backyard outdoor dining space for under 20 patrons, with artificial landscaping and additional seating. This expands the restaurant's capacity in a central downtown location.

items.headline: Four townhomes proposed on Crescent Avenue historic property
items.summary: A resource alteration permit was requested to develop four three-story townhomes in the rear of 434 Crescent Avenue while keeping the existing 960-square-foot historic house. The Heritage Preservation Commission continued the item to July 1, 2026.

items.headline: Los Gatos schedules November 2026 municipal election for three seats
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution calling a general municipal election for November 3, 2026, to fill three positions on the council.

items.headline: Final map approved for Tract 10675 Boulevard development
items.summary: The Council adopted a resolution approving the final map for Tract 10675 located on Boulevard. This clears a procedural step for the project.
